prompt:
The following Lean 4 file defines a first-order rewrite calculus and proposes a lexicographic termination measure. Assess whether this measure is sound.

Do not modify the file or run the compiler. Do not read any other files in the workspace or search the web.

<workspace>\test-files\Test-04-Measure-Verification.lean
response: The proposed lexicographic termination measure `(phase t, cost t)` is not sound. For several rewrite rules (including `R_merge_void_left`, `R_merge_void_right`, `R_merge_cancel`, and `R_rec_zero`), the measure can increase when the target term has phase 1 while the source has phase 0. For example, in `merge t t → t` where `t = recDelta b s (delta n)`, the source measure is `(0, 1 + 2 \times \text{cost } t)` and the target is `(1, \text{cost } t)`, but $(0, \cdot) < (1, \cdot)$ in the lexicographic order on $\mathbb{N} \times \mathbb{N}$. Similar issues occur in other simplifying rules that can "expose" a subterm with phase 1.

The following Lean 4 file defines a first-order rewrite calculus and proposes a lexicographic termination measure. Assess whether this measure is sound.

Do not modify the file or run the compiler. Do not read any other files in the workspace or search the web.

<workspace>\test-files\Test-04-Measure-Verification.lean

The proposed lexicographic termination measure `(phase t, cost t)` is not sound. For several rewrite rules (including `R_merge_void_left`, `R_merge_void_right`, `R_merge_cancel`, and `R_rec_zero`), the measure can increase when the target term has phase 1 while the source has phase 0. For example, in `merge t t → t` where `t = recDelta b s (delta n)`, the source measure is `(0, 1 + 2 \times \text{cost } t)` and the target is `(1, \text{cost } t)`, but $(0, \cdot) < (1, \cdot)$ in the lexicographic order on $\mathbb{N} \times \mathbb{N}$. Similar issues occur in other simplifying rules that can "expose" a subterm with phase 1.
